Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff?S Proofs Of Service Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 418.10
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANTS LLOYD DAMON, MATTHEW OERTLI, SHAGUN ARORA, FEIFEI XUE, NICOLE APPELLE, MATTHEW ALDRICH, MIGUEL CEREJO, UCSF HEALTH ACO, LLC, UCSF HEALTH COMMUNITY HOSPITALS, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, and UCSF MEDICAL FOUNDATION's Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Proofs Of Service Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 418.10.

No Appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Eleven specially appearing individuals and entities' motion to quash plaintiff's proofs of service pursuant to CCP 418.10 is denied in its entirety. This motion, assertedly brought "out of an abundance of caution to preclude improper entry of default judgments against" the moving parties, is itself improper. The title of the motion invokes CCP 418.10 as the authority for the motion yet that code section says nothing about a motion to quash proofs of service nor is the court aware of any authority that provides for such relief. If and when a party believes that he, she or it was served with a summons impermissibly, then a section 418.10 motion may be appropriate, but none of the moving parties appear to contend that they have been served with a summons. Moreover, since the moving parties are appearing specially and a special appearance is confined to challenging the court's jurisdiction, the moving parties may not raise any issue about the effect of the request for dismissal filed by the plaintiff on July 10, 2023, at least without their appearances being treated as general appearances acceding to the court's jurisdiction.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/HK)
